title: Modals of obligation and advice
explanation:
Modals of obligation and advice

must / have to express obligation, mustn't expresses prohibition, don't have to means no necessity, and should / ought to express advice.

questions.text: You [*mustn't|don't have to|shouldn't to] park here. It is illegal.
I [*have to|mustn't|should] wear a uniform at work.
You [*don't have to|mustn't|ought] come if you are busy.
He [*should|must|has to] see a dentist about that tooth.
We [*had to|must|should] cancel the trip because of the storm.

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: Students ____ hand in the project by Friday. | *have to | should | don't have to
You ____ tell anyone. It is a secret. | *mustn't | don't have to | ought
questions.feedback: You are not allowed to tell anyone.

questions.text: We ____ hurry; there is plenty of time. | *don't have to | mustn't | had to
She ____ eat less sugar if she wants to feel better. | *should | mustn't | has to not
Passengers [*must show|must showing|show must] their tickets.
You [*ought to apologise|ought apologise|ought apologising] for what you said.
I [*didn't have to|mustn't|shouldn't] work yesterday because it was a public holiday.
They [*have to get|must getting|should to get] up early on weekdays.
You [*should not ignore|must not to ignore|ought ignore] his advice.
